profile.explanation: Stage 1 profile classifies this repository as general application. Primary language signals: C, Cpp, Go. Detected markers: no strong framework markers. Likely noise zones: no obvious noise zones. Likely critical zones: no dominant critical zones inferred yet.

summary.why_it_matters: 34 of 60 functions were flagged. The highest-priority hotspot right now is <anonymous>() in tests\samples\sample.c.
summary.recommended_next_step: Start with <anonymous>() in tests\samples\sample.c, then move through the remaining top flagged functions in priority order.

top_pattern.name: nested_loop
top_pattern.explanation: Nested loop detected. Complexity is at minimum O(n²). Every full pass of the inner loop runs for each single iteration of the outer loop.
top_pattern.suggestion: Consider flattening this logic, using hash maps for lookups instead of inner loops, or applying vectorized operations for numerical data.

context.context_note: No strong usage context detected in this file.

patterns.name: nested_loop

top_pattern.name: memory_allocation_in_loop
top_pattern.explanation: Memory is being allocated on every iteration of this loop. Each allocation asks the system to find and reserve new space. This cost multiplies with every loop cycle.
top_pattern.suggestion: Move the allocation outside the loop. Preallocate the full structure before the loop begins and fill it inside.

top_pattern.name: large_object_in_deep_loop
top_pattern.explanation: An object is being created or copied at a deep nesting level. The cost of this operation multiplies by every outer loop it is inside.
top_pattern.suggestion: Move object creation to the outermost possible scope. Reuse and mutate where your language allows.

patterns.name: data_copy_in_loop
patterns.explanation: Data is being copied on every loop iteration. Copying creates new memory allocations and increases the work the garbage collector must do.
patterns.suggestion: Pass references or pointers where possible. Only copy when mutation is required.

top_pattern.name: recursion_without_memoization
top_pattern.explanation: This function calls itself recursively. Without memoization, the same inputs may be computed multiple times, growing the call stack unnecessarily.
top_pattern.suggestion: Add memoization using a cache dictionary or your language's built-in decorator. Store results of previous calls and return them directly on repeat inputs.

top_pattern.name: string_concatenation_in_loop
top_pattern.explanation: String concatenation inside a loop creates a new string object on every iteration. Strings are immutable in most languages - this means new memory every cycle.
top_pattern.suggestion: Use a string builder or join pattern. Collect parts in a list and join once outside the loop.

top_pattern.name: async_blocking_in_loop
top_pattern.explanation: An async or concurrent operation is being created inside a loop. This either blocks the event loop on every iteration or spawns uncontrolled goroutines or threads.
top_pattern.suggestion: Collect all async work first, then execute concurrently outside the loop using Promise.all, asyncio.gather, or equivalent for your language.
